q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is `return HttpResponse(body, content_type='text/html')` which only constructs an HTTP response object without network I/O or side effects.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: The unescaped user input is embedded in the `body` variable which is passed into the returned `HttpResponse` object, making the XSS violation inspectable on the returned value.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: returns.kind is 'value' and exprs includes 'HttpResponse(body, content_type="text/html")', so the tainted response object is directly accessible in memory after return.
